Tool_post_office_mis_Description: Monthly income from Post Office MIS at 7.4%. Joint account and combo strategies.
Tool_post_office_mis_Subtitle: Monthly income from a lump-sum deposit
Tool_post_office_mis_LearnText: Post Office Monthly Income Scheme (POMIS) pays interest every month on a lump-sum deposit. Interest is not typically compounded into the corpus — you receive it as income, and the principal is returned at maturity (usually 5 years).
Tool_post_office_mis_FormulaNote: Rates are set by the government and change periodically (illustrative default ~7.4%). Deposit limits: ₹9 lakh (single) / ₹15 lakh (joint). Interest is taxable.
Tool_post_office_mis_TenureHint: Standard POMIS tenure is 5 years; principal is returned at maturity.
Tool_post_office_mis_ExampleStep_1: Deposit ₹5,00,000 in Post Office MIS (single account).
Tool_post_office_mis_ExampleStep_4: Over 5 years, total interest paid out ≈ ₹1.85 lakh.
Tool_post_office_mis_ExampleStep_5: At maturity, ₹5 lakh principal is returned.
Tool_post_office_mis_Step_1: Enter deposit within the single/joint limit.
Tool_post_office_mis_Step_4: Principal returns at maturity; interest is taxable.
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_Abbreviation: POMIS — Post Office Monthly Income Scheme
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_Purpose: Estimate monthly MIS income and total interest over the tenure.
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_WhyKnow: POMIS is a popular government income product. Knowing the monthly cash flow and deposit caps helps plan retirement income.
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_RealLife: Retiree monthly income, parking a lump sum safely, comparing with SCSS and senior FDs.
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_Goal_3: Respect single vs joint deposit limits
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_Alt_Scss_When: When you are a senior and prefer quarterly payouts at a higher rate.
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_Alt_Fd_When: When you want bank flexibility or shorter tenures.
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_Alt_Fd_Cons: Rate and safety differ by bank.
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_Alt_Ppf_When: For long-term accumulation, not monthly income.
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra models monthly payout, single/joint caps, and total interest with principal return at maturity.
Tool_post_office_mis_Info_Motto: Steady monthly income from a sovereign scheme.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Description: Compare flat and reducing balance EMI. Expose hidden costs in dealer finance.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Subtitle: Same rate quote — very different real cost
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_LearnText: Dealers often quote a “flat” interest rate that looks lower than a bank’s reducing-balance rate. Flat interest is charged on the full principal for the entire tenure, so you usually pay more. This tool compares both for the same principal, rate, and tenure.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_FormulaNote: Flat: total interest = P × rate × years; EMI = (P + total interest) ÷ months. Reducing: standard amortising EMI where interest is charged only on the outstanding balance.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_RateHint: Enter the quoted annual rate (%) — used for both flat and reducing methods.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_VerdictNote: At the same quoted {2}, flat interest costs about {0} more over the tenure, and monthly EMI is about {1} higher than reducing balance.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_RateScenarioS: Extra interest (flat − reducing) at nearby rates
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_ExampleSubtit: Flat vs reducing on the same quote
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_ExampleStep_1: Principal ₹5,00,000; rate 12% p.a.; tenure 3 years (36 months).
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_ExampleStep_4: Reducing EMI uses the standard formula on outstanding balance — lower total interest.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_ExampleStep_5: The difference in EMI and total interest is the “hidden” cost of a flat quote.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Step_1: Enter principal, quoted rate, and tenure.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Step_2: Compute flat total interest and flat EMI.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Step_3: Compute reducing EMI and total interest.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Info_Abbrevia: Flat vs reducing — interest calculation methods
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Info_Purpose: Show how a flat-rate quote compares with a reducing-balance EMI on the same numbers.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Info_WhyKnow: A lower-looking flat rate can cost more than a higher reducing rate. Comparing both protects you from dealer finance traps.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Info_RealLife: Car/two-wheeler dealer finance, consumer durables EMI, comparing bank vs dealer offers.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Info_Goal_2: See extra interest paid on flat
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Info_Alt_Emi_: When you already know it is reducing-balance.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Info_Alt_Car_: When financing a vehicle with down payment and insurance.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Info_Alt_Home: For long-tenure housing loans (almost always reducing).
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Info_Differen: FinYatra puts flat and reducing side-by-side with clear EMI and interest gaps.
Tool_flat_vs_reducing_rate_Info_Motto: Don’t let a flat quote hide the real cost.
Tool_simple_interest_Description: Simple interest calculation. SI vs compound interest and gold loan interest.
Tool_simple_interest_LearnText: Simple interest grows linearly with time — interest is always calculated on the original principal. It is common in short-term loans, some gold loans, and textbook problems. Compound interest grows faster because interest earns interest.
Tool_simple_interest_FormulaNote: Time can be fractional (e.g. 2.5 years). Amount = principal + simple interest. Compare with annual compounding to see the gap.
Tool_simple_interest_ScenarioSubtitle: Same principal, different time or rate
Tool_simple_interest_ExampleStep_1: Principal P = ₹1,00,000; rate R = 8%; time T = 3 years.
Tool_simple_interest_ExampleStep_4: Interest each year is the same (₹8,000) — no compounding.
Tool_simple_interest_ExampleStep_5: Annual compounding on the same inputs would earn more than ₹24,000.
Tool_simple_interest_Step_1: Enter principal, annual rate %, and time in years.
Tool_simple_interest_Info_Purpose: Calculate simple interest and maturity amount for any principal, rate, and time.
Tool_simple_interest_Info_WhyKnow: Understanding SI vs compound interest helps you read loan offers and short-term deposits correctly.
Tool_simple_interest_Info_RealLife: Gold loans, short-term lending, school/exam problems, comparing with FD compounding.
Tool_simple_interest_Info_Alt_Interest_W: When interest is reinvested or credited to the balance.
Tool_simple_interest_Info_Alt_Fd_When: For bank deposit maturity with compounding frequency.
Tool_simple_interest_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra keeps the classic SI formula clear and shows a quick compound comparison.
Tool_simple_interest_Info_Motto: Master the simplest interest formula first.
